goal_statement: Build a data-driven decision support system with the sole objective of improving project delivery certainty through GitHub-integrated Scrum project management dashboard

success_criteria.criteria: Real-time synchronization with GitHub without data loss
Clear, actionable burndown charts and efficiency metrics visualization
Measurable improvement in project estimation accuracy through member efficiency analysis
Single-command deployment to shared hosting with automated CI/CD

kpis.kpi: API response time < 200ms for dashboard updates
Zero data corruption during GitHub sync operations
Team members actively use and update time tracking data

constraints.constraint: Read-only access to GitHub data (unidirectional sync only)
Deployment must work on shared hosting environments with resource limitations
Must use lightweight database solutions (text/embedded databases)
System architecture must pursue rapid development and easy maintenance

assumptions.assumption: Team members will consistently update remaining time daily for accurate burndown charts
GitHub API rate limits will not significantly impact synchronization frequency
Shared hosting environment will provide sufficient resources for team-scale operations
Historical data preservation will provide valuable insights for long-term project analysis

open_questions.question: What is the optimal synchronization frequency to balance real-time updates with GitHub API rate limits?
How should the system handle estimation accuracy training and calibration for team members?
What fallback mechanisms are needed when GitHub API is unavailable or rate-limited?
How much historical data should be retained and what are the storage implications?
